title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Contract Lens: Result Values
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts result values increment in an exhaustive asynchronous state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
